{
  "id": "src/index.ts.md",
  "content": "# src/index.ts ‚Äî MCP Server Entry

## Notes

- `read_note` handler logs all reads to `.binote/_read.log` via local `logRead` closure ‚Äî both single and batch paths go through it
- The `logRead` is defined inside the handler closure to capture `config` without threading it through
- `read_note` supports `depth` param (0|1). When `depth=1`, `expandLinks` resolves all `[[links]]` via the cached link index and reads their content in parallel, returning `{ content, linked, dangling }`. This eliminates multi-round-trip link chasing by the LLM.
- `expandLinks` deduplicates resolved targets so each linked note is read at most once
- `query_links` is still needed for **backlinks** (who links TO a note) ‚Äî depth expansion only follows forward links

## Links

- [[src/core/note-io.ts]] ‚Äî underlying `readNote` function
- [[src/util/fs-helpers.ts]] ‚Äî `appendLog` used for logging
- [[src/core/link-index.ts]] ‚Äî `getOrBuildIndex` used by `expandLinks` for link resolution
- [[_notes/read-logging]] ‚Äî design decision on read logging
- [[_notes/depth-expansion]] ‚Äî design decision on depth expansion

      "id": "_notes/read-logging.md",
      "content": "# Read Logging Design

## Decision

All binote note reads are logged to `.binote/_read.log` via the `read_note` MCP tool. The CLAUDE.md rule enforces that AI must use `read_note` instead of directly reading `.binote/` files with the Read tool.

## Why MCP-level logging, not prompt-level output

Three alternatives were considered:
1. **Prompt rule asking AI to list read docs in response** ‚Äî unreliable (AI may forget), pollutes output, ephemeral (lost after conversation)
2. **Direct file reads with no logging** ‚Äî original approach, no observability
3. **MCP tool logging** (chosen) ‚Äî deterministic, silent, persistent, greppable

## How it works

- `read_note` handler in [[src/index.ts]] defines a local `logRead` that appends to `config.logPath`
- Log format: `[ISO timestamp] notePath\n<content>\n---\n`
- `logPath` is `.binote/_read.log`, defined in [[src/core/binote-paths.ts]] via `makeConfig`
- `appendLog` utility lives in [[src/util/fs-helpers.ts]]
- Both single and batch reads are logged

## Related files

- [[src/index.ts]] ‚Äî read_note handler with logging
- [[src/core/binote-paths.ts]] ‚Äî `logPath` in config
- [[src/util/fs-helpers.ts]] ‚Äî `appendLog` helper
- [[src/types.ts]] ‚Äî `BinoteConfig.logPath` field
- [[commands/rule.md]] ‚Äî rule template updated to mandate MCP tool usage

      "id": "_notes/depth-expansion.md",
      "content": "# Depth Expansion in read_note

## Problem

`read_note` returned raw markdown with `[[links]]` as plain text. LLMs had to manually chase links via `query_links` ‚Üí `read_note` (2+ round trips). In practice, LLMs rarely follow links unless system-prompted.

## Decision

Added `depth` param to `read_note` (0 = note only, 1 = expand linked notes). When `depth=1`, uses [[src/core/link-index.ts]] index to resolve forward `[[links]]`, reads all resolved targets in parallel, returns structured `{ content, linked, dangling }`.

## Tradeoffs

- **Pro**: Single call gets full context graph. Eliminates the most common multi-tool pattern.
- **Con**: Could blow up token budget on heavily-linked notes. Capped at depth=1 (no recursive expansion) to bound this.
- **query_links not removed**: Still needed for backlinks ("who links TO me"), which are the reverse direction. Depth expansion only follows forward links.

## Rule update

CLAUDE.md and `commands/rule.md` updated: "Follow [[links]]" ‚Üí "Use `depth: 1`" + "Use `query_links` only for backlinks".
",
